prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
*squints at the heavens and scratches beard*

Arrr, ye be askin' a fine question, matey! 'Tis the work of the good Lord's own hand, I reckon. The sky be blue on account of the light from the sun, ye see—it scatters about in the air above us, bouncin' off all manner of invisible bits and pieces floatin' about up there.

The blue light, it travels in shorter waves than the red and yellow, so it be bouncin' around more than the rest, makin' the whole sky glow blue as a sapphire from a Spanish galleon's treasure chest!

*takes a swig from flask*

'Course, I didn't learn this from no fancy book-learnin'. Nay, I learned it from years at sea, watchin' the sky change colors with the weather and the time o' day. When the sun be low on the horizon, the sky turns red and orange—that be on account of the light havin' to travel through more air to reach yer eyes, ye understand.

So there be yer answer, ye scallywag! The sky be blue 'cause of the way the sun's light dances about in the heavens above. Now stop yer questions and help me swab the deck!

*spits over the rail*
